mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures and signals weakening consumer demand, causing equity markets to price in lower corporate profit growth and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates a stagflationary demand shock that erodes real purchasing power and compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and falling RSAFS fails to predict negative returns with an accuracy exceeding 55%, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income and supports consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1300 thousand units, it signals a severe contraction in construction activity and downstream consumer durables demand, forcing equity analysts to downgrade earnings forecasts and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 1300 are followed by positive 60-day returns in >65% of observations, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising federal funds rates and persistently high consumer price inflation creates a hostile macro environment that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy longer, increasing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL above 300 fails to predict negative returns with an out-of-sample accuracy above 58%, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, synchronized expansion in industrial production and real GDP confirms broad-based economic strengthening, reducing recession risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Synchronized positive growth in INDPRO and GDPC1 during recovery phases yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance levels.
